lineage.Process: CalculateField NSWMapV3_03b_class_NC ClassName 'Eastern Riverine Forests' PYTHON_9.3 #

Merge blank_NSWMap;NSWMapV3_03b_class_SC \\espresso\nswmap\nswmap\Publications\NSWMap3_03_ClassesVector_RFS_b\NSWVegetationClasses_V3_03b_SC.gdb\NSWVeg_GDA94LCC\NSWMapV3_03b_class_SC2 "MapName "MapName" true true false 255 Text 0 0 ,First,#,blank_NSWMap,MapName,-1,-1,NSWMapV3_03b_class_SC,MapName,-1,-1;ClassNumber "ClassNumber" true true false 2 Short 0 0 ,First,#,blank_NSWMap,ClassNumber,-1,-1,NSWMapV3_03b_class_SC,NSWMAPV3,-1,-1;ClassName "ClassName" true true false 255 Text 0 0 ,First,#,blank_NSWMap,ClassName,-1,-1,NSWMapV3_03b_class_SC,Class,-1,-1;FormationNumber "FormationNumber" true true false 2 Short 0 0 ,First,#,blank_NSWMap,FormationNumber,-1,-1,NSWMapV3_03b_class_SC,FormNum,-1,-1;FormationName "FormationName" true true false 255 Text 0 0 ,First,#,blank_NSWMap,FormationName,-1,-1,NSWMapV3_03b_class_SC,Formation_1,-1,-1;VIS_ID "VIS_ID" true true false 8 Double 0 0 ,First,#,blank_NSWMap,VIS_ID,-1,-1,NSWMapV3_03b_class_SC,VIS_ID,-1,-1;ClassificationSkill "ClassificationSkill" true true false 255 Text 0 0 ,First,#,blank_NSWMap,ClassificationSkill,-1,-1,NSWMapV3_03b_class_SC,ClassificationSkill,-1,-1;ThematicResolution "ThematicResolution" true true false 255 Text 0 0 ,First,#,blank_NSWMap,ThematicResolution,-1,-1,NSWMapV3_03b_class_SC,ThematicResolution,-1,-1;SpatialResolution "SpatialResolution" true true false 255 Text 0 0 ,First,#,blank_NSWMap,SpatialResolution,-1,-1,NSWMapV3_03b_class_SC,SpatialResolutionNew,-1,-1;MapReliability "MapReliability" true true false 255 Text 0 0 ,First,#,blank_NSWMap,MapReliability,-1,-1,NSWMapV3_03b_class_SC,MapReliability,-1,-1;Currency "Currency" true true false 255 Text 0 0 ,First,#,blank_NSWMap,Currency,-1,-1,NSWMapV3_03b_class_SC,Currency_,-1,-1;Shape_Length "Shape_Length" false true true 8 Double 0 0 ,First,#,blank_NSWMap,Shape_Length,-1,-1,NSWMapV3_03b_class_SC,Shape_Length,-1,-1;Shape_Area "Shape_Area" false true true 8 Double 0 0 ,First,#,blank_NSWMap,Shape_Area,-1,-1,NSWMapV3_03b_class_SC,Shape_Area,-1,-1"

dataIdInfo.envirDesc: Microsoft Windows 7 Version 6.1 (Build 7601) Service Pack 1; ESRI ArcGIS 10.0.4.4000
dataIdInfo.idPurp: Vegetation Classes of NSW (version 3.03b) - David A. Keith & Christopher C. Simpson. 27th June 2012
dataIdInfo.idAbs:
Vegetation Classes of NSW (version 3.03b) - David A. Keith & Christopher C. Simpson. 27th June 2012

This vegetation map shows the extant distributions of vegetation classesthroughout NSW, and provides users with information about the resolution, currency and uncertainties in the underlying data that were used to assemble the map. Data represents NSW native vegetation extent, compiled from various vegetation maps using methods outlined in Simpson et al. (2011) and in Keith and Simpson (2010). The NSW vegetation map (version 2.2, Keith and Simpson 2006) was revised by interpreting additional candidate maps as vector layers and synthesising these into a single vector-based data set. This involved eight steps: • developing a comprehensive ‘standard’ classification of vegetation classesfor NSW; • collating and standardising the projection and format of candidate source maps; • assigning vegetation units of source maps to NSW vegetation classes; • assessing the spatial resolution, currency and reliability of candidate source maps; • assembling a composite map from candidate source maps to maximise reliability; • applying a spatial mask to represent extant native vegetation; • adjusting spatial resolution by dissolving small polygons; • attributing the spatial resolution, currency and reliability of the underlying source data sets. The classification of 106vegetation classes described by Keith (2004) was adopted as the framework for preparation of version 3.03of the NSW vegetation map. Polygons from the “Estuarine macrophytes CCA” dataset of less than 0.1 ha were eliminated. For all other datasets polygons of less than 2 ha were eliminated.The map incorporates data from a statewide woody vegetation mask from the NSW Woody Vegetation Change Detection Program (Kitchen et al. 2010).The map is presented as four feature classes within an ESRI ArcGIS (9.3) geodatabase. Supersedes Keith and Simpson (2006), Keith (2004) and Pressey et al. (2000). Pressey et al. (2000) was the native veg extent product used to calculate native veg cover values for the Over-Cleared Landscapes Database prior to July 2006.

References: Keith D. A. (2004) Ocean Shores to Desert Dunes: The native vegetation of New South Wales and the ACT. Department of Environment and Conservation, Sydney. Keith, D. A. and Simpson, C. C. (2010) Vegetation Formations of NSW (version 3.0): A seamless map for modelling fire spread and behaviour. Report to the Rural Fire Service. NSW Department of Environment & Climate Change. October 2010. Keith, D. A. and Simpson, C. C. (2006). A protocol for assessment and integration of vegetation maps, with an application to spatial data sets from south-eastern Australia. Austral Ecology 33, 761–774. Kitchen, J., Clark, A., Danaher, T., Armston, J. and Scarth, P. (2010). Operational use of annual Landsat-5 TM and Landsat-7 ETM+ image timeseries for mapping wooded extent and foliage projective cover in north-eastern Australia. In: Proceedings of the 15th Australasian Remote Sensing and Photogrammetry Conference. Alice Springs. Pressey, R.L., Hager, T.C., Ryan, K.M., Schwarz, J., Wall, S., Ferrier, S. and Creaser, P.M. (2000). Using abiotic data for conservation assessments over extensive regions: quantitative methods applied across New South Wales, Australia. Biological Conservation 96, 55-82. Simpson, C. C., Miller, I. and Keith, D. A. (2011) Vegetation Formations of NSW (version 3.02). Report to the Rural Fire Service. NSW Office of Environment & Heritage. May 2011.
dataIdInfo.idCredit: David A. Keith & Christopher C. Simpson. NSW Office of Environment & Heritage

attrdomv.udom: Sequential unique whole numbers that are automatically generated.

attr.attrdef: Length of feature in internal units.

attrdomv.udom: Positive real numbers that are automatically generated.

attr.attrdef: Area of feature in internal units squared.

attrdomv.udom: Positive real numbers that are automatically generated.
